sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Move blue block UP to be adjacent to right red block vertically.
- Plan: UP x3 to rows 24-27 (same as right red block), but that would overlap. Try UP x2 to rows 32-35 (just below right red at rows 24-27, with 4 rows gap). Actually, UP x3 would put blue at rows 24-27, which overlaps with right red. Let me try UP x2 to rows 32-35, then SPACE. Wait, actually the right red block is at rows 24-27. If I move the blue block UP 3 times (12 rows), it would be at rows 24-27, which is the same as the right red block. That would cause a collision. Let me try UP x2 to rows 32-35, which is 8 rows below the right red block. Then SPACE. Actually, let me try UP x3 to rows 24-27 and see what happens. If it collides with the right red block, maybe the game handles it somehow.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 23 of about 58 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
UP x3 and SPACE didn't complete the level. Let me check the current position of the blue block. The blue block was at rows 36-39, and I moved it UP 3 times (12 rows), so it should be at rows 24-27. But the right red block is also at rows 24-27, cols 28-39. So the blue block might have collided with the right red block.
Let me check the current positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'R', 'Y', 'M', 'c'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"{n['color']}: row=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}], col=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After UP x3 and SPACE:
- Blue block: rows 24-27, cols 28-39 (moved to the position of the right red block)
- Right red block: rows 24-27, cols 20-27 (moved LEFT to cols 20-27, pixels reduced from 48 to 32)
- Left red block: rows 16-19, cols 8-19 (unchanged)
So the blue block replaced the right red block, and the right red block moved LEFT and shrunk. The blue block is now at rows 24-27, cols 28-39, which is the same position as the right red block was initially.
This is similar to what happened before when I moved the blue block UP and pressed SPACE. The blue block seems to "snap" to the position of the right red block, and the right red block moves LEFT.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The blue block is now at rows 24-27, cols 28-39. The right red block is at rows 24-27, cols 20-27.
Maybe the goal is to move the blue block to a position where it's aligned with the tower (cols 40-43). The blue block is at cols 28-39, and the tower is at cols 40-43. So the blue block is just to the left of the tower.
Let me try moving the blue block RIGHT to be aligned with the tower. From cols 28-39 to cols 40-51 is 12 cols RIGHT (3 RIGHT moves). Then SPACE.
But wait, the tower is at cols 40-43, which is 4 cols wide. The blue block is 12 cols wide. If I move the blue block RIGHT 3 times, it would be at cols 40-51, which overlaps with the tower at cols 40-43.
Let me try RIGHT x3, SPACE.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- After UP x3 and SPACE, blue block moved to rows 24-27, cols 28-39 (replacing right red block).
- Right red block moved LEFT to cols 20-27 and shrunk.
- Blue block is now adjacent to tower (cols 40-43).
- Hypothesis: Move blue block RIGHT to align with tower, then SPACE.
Plan: RIGHT x3 to cols 40-51 (overlapping with tower at cols 40-43), then SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move blue block RIGHT 3 times to cols 40-51, then SPACE
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
